title: SICP 5.5 compiler targeting the register-machine simulator
goal: Implement a runnable SICP 5.5 compiler probe that compiles representative Scheme procedures into register-machine instruction sequences and executes them through the simulator.
next_actions: Add tests/sicp/ch5_compiler.esk with the SICP instruction-sequence representation.
Implement compile dispatch for self-evaluating expressions, quoted expressions, variables, assignments, definitions, conditionals, sequences, lambda, and applications.
Implement preserving, append-instruction-sequences, register-needed/modified tracking, and linkage handling.
Run compiled procedures through the existing register-machine simulator, including arithmetic, conditionals, lexical closures, and recursive factorial.
Add performance/robustness stress after the canonical compiler examples pass.
acceptance: tests/sicp/ch5_compiler.esk exists and self-checks the SICP 5.5 compiler.
The probe passes under AOT compile/run.
ICC sicp-completeness observes sicp_ch5_compiler_r and sicp_ch5_compiler_aot as PASS.
status_note: Implemented on PR #103 as tests/sicp/ch5_compiler.esk. Focused JIT and AOT checks pass for instruction-sequence needs/modifies/statements, preserving/linkage, compile dispatch, compiled primitive calls, conditionals, definitions, mutation, closures, recursive factorial, and reusable compiled code. Awaiting full SICP smoke/ICC gate before marking done.
ux_payoff: Completes the book's compiler arc and proves Eshkol can express and run SICP's metacircular-to-machine compilation pipeline.
